steps.name: Implement extractHeadingAnchoredSnippet and wire call sites
steps.description: Add extractHeadingAnchoredSnippet(content, concern): string | null to src/core/concern-matcher.ts per architecture-design.md contract: detect ATX markdown headings (regex ^#{1,6}\s), partition content into sections from each heading to the next heading of equal-or-higher level, return null if no headings exist, return the single matching section if exactly one matches, return the highest-occurrence-count section if multiple match (ties broken by first-occurring section). Wire into the three snippet-building call sites inside matchConcernInSnapshot (feature files, ADR files, CLAUDE.md): call extractHeadingAnchoredSnippet first, fall back to file.content on null, then pass through the existing capSnippetAtHeadingBoundary truncation unchanged. Covers all 5 scenarios in heading-anchored-extraction.feature.
criteria: A concern matching exactly one section of a multi-section file returns a snippet scoped to that section only
A concern in a headingless file returns the unchanged whole-file-up-to-cap snippet (zero regression)
A concern appearing only in a heading line anchors the snippet starting at that heading
A concern appearing in multiple sections returns the section with the highest occurrence count
A matched section exceeding the size cap is truncated with a warning, reusing the existing truncation mechanism
steps.scenario_name: Snippet is narrowed to the matched section in a multi-section file

steps.name: Fix ancestor-section-always-wins density bug
steps.description: Post-merge dogfood check found that a section's structural ancestor (e.g. a single top-level H1 title heading followed by multiple H2 subsections, with no second H1) always wins the multi-section density comparison over its own descendants, because its occurrence count sums every nested subsection's occurrences. Fix extractHeadingAnchoredSnippet in src/core/concern-matcher.ts: before density comparison, filter out any matching section that is a structural ancestor of another matching section (i.e. whose line range fully contains another matching section's line range). Density comparison then applies only among matching sections with no ancestor/descendant relationship to each other. See docs/feature/heading-anchored-snippets/distill/upstream-issues.md Issue 1 for full analysis and acceptance criteria.
criteria: A wave-decisions.md with one top-level H1 title and three ## subsections, where the concern appears in exactly one subsection, returns a snippet scoped to that subsection, not the whole file
The returned snippet does not start at the top-level title heading
All 5 pre-existing scenarios in heading-anchored-extraction.feature remain green (zero regression)
steps.scenario_name: A document title heading does not steal the match from its own subsection
